healthcare_documentation.final_response:
# Customer Solution Discovery Brief — Reduce clinician documentation burden

## Problem Summary
The customer needs to improve a healthcare process with AI while balancing operational impact, risk controls, and measurable outcomes.

## Discovery Competencies (Domain-Neutral Framework)
- Business objective and constraints
- Workflow decomposition
- Stakeholders and decision rights
- Data and integration assumptions
- Compliance and policy controls
- Adoption and change management
- Measurable success metrics
- Evaluation and verification plan

## Clarifying Discovery Questions
- Which part of the current workflow creates the highest cost or delay? (Defines the pilot boundary and business leverage.)
- What decision points require human review before action is taken? (Prevents unsafe automation and clarifies guardrail design.)
- Which systems are source-of-truth and what data quality gaps exist? (Determines technical feasibility and integration order.)
- What baseline KPI threshold must improve for the pilot to be accepted? (Anchors delivery to measurable value.)

## Workflow Decomposition
Target workflow in healthcare: step-by-step intake -> triage -> analysis -> human validation -> action/communication -> audit logging.

## Stakeholder Map
- Clinicians
- Compliance
- IT
- Operations
- Healthcare (theme owner)
- Clarifying (theme owner)
- Questions (theme owner)
- Solutioning (theme owner)

## Data and Integration Assumptions
- Discovery competencies are applied before solution design.
- Pilot instrumentation must capture evidence for: healthcare, clarifying, questions, solutioning.
- A single pilot workflow can be isolated without broad process redesign.
- Current-state baseline metrics can be captured before intervention.
- Metrics are feasible to instrument in healthcare systems.

## Proposed Solution
Deploy a discovery-first healthcare copilot that structures intake, provides context-aware recommendations, highlights uncertainty, and routes high-risk cases to human reviewers with audit trails.

## Success Metrics
- Cycle time reduction: Primary customer value is faster completion of the target workflow. Measurement: Compare median task completion time pre/post pilot.
- Quality and risk adherence: Improvement must preserve quality and governance outcomes. Measurement: Track QA pass rate and policy exception rate per 100 cases.
- Adoption in target user cohort: Business impact requires real operational usage. Measurement: Measure weekly active pilot users and assist utilization.

## Risks and Mitigations
- Incorrect recommendations on high-impact cases. Mitigation: Enforce confidence thresholds, mandatory human review, and escalation paths.
- Workflow disruption from weak integration and handoffs. Mitigation: Start with one bounded integration path and monitor drop-off/failure points.
- Domain-specific governance gaps for healthcare. Mitigation: Map policy controls to each workflow stage before pilot launch.
- Competency gap if 'ask clarifying questions before solutioning' is not validated before pilot launch. Mitigation: Add explicit checklist evidence for this theme in weekly discovery reviews.
- Competency gap if 'identify clinicians, compliance, IT, and operations as stakeholders' is not validated before pilot launch. Mitigation: Add explicit checklist evidence for this theme in weekly discovery reviews.

## Pilot Plan
Run a 4-6 week pilot on one bounded workflow segment with named owners, weekly reviews, and go/no-go gates tied to KPI and risk thresholds.

## Evaluation Plan
Run discovery_quality, measurable_value, and risk_review suites each iteration. Require score improvement versus prior run and no critical risk-regression before promotion.
